input.title: Personal Finance and Budgeting App
input.description: Personal Finance and Budgeting App is a web-based enterprise platform that centralizes operational workflows, data visibility, and cross-functional reporting.

user_classes.Admin: Configures the system, manages users, and oversees permissions and settings.
user_classes.Manager: Oversees departmental workflows, approvals, KPIs, and operational reporting.
user_classes.Employee: Performs day-to-day operational tasks within assigned modules and permissions.
user_classes.User: Generic authenticated platform user with access limited by role and department.

definitions.Admin: System user with full administrative privileges.
definitions.Manager: Operational owner of a department, module, or workflow.
definitions.Employee: End user performing role-specific business operations.

constraints: Initial release will focus on the web platform only
Role-based access control is mandatory across all modules
Operational data must be auditable and retained according to policy

operating_environment.frontend: Web-based interface built with React Native and accessible through modern browsers.
operating_environment.backend: Service layer implemented with Node.js, PostgreSQL for business operations.
operating_environment.deployment: Cloud-hosted environment with centralized monitoring, backups, and automated deployments.

NFR-01.title: Security and Access Control
requirements: All sensitive data must be encrypted in transit and at rest
Role-based authorization must restrict access by module and action
All authentication events and privileged actions must be logged

NFR-02.title: Performance and Scalability
requirements: Core pages and dashboards should load within acceptable enterprise SLAs
The platform must support concurrent usage across multiple departments
Background jobs and reporting workloads should not block transactional workflows

NFR-03.title: Reliability and Recoverability
requirements: Automated backups must run on a defined schedule
Critical workflows should support retry-safe processing and audit logging
Operational alerts should be generated for failures and degraded services

system_attributes.reliability: Platform should provide resilient transactional processing with backup and recovery capabilities.
system_attributes.scalability: Architecture should support horizontal scaling for APIs, background workers, and reporting services.
system_attributes.security: Authentication, authorization, encryption, and auditability must be built into every module.
system_attributes.maintainability: Codebase should remain modular, observable, and safe to extend through clear service boundaries.

out_of_scope: Native mobile applications in the first release
Large third-party marketplace or legacy ERP migrations unless explicitly scoped
Non-core experimental modules outside the platform's operational workflows

FR-01.title: User Registration and Security
requirements: Users must be able to complete user registration and security through the approved entry points provided by the platform.
The system must validate submitted credentials, identity data, and required fields before allowing user registration and security to continue.
User Registration and Security must enforce secure handling of sensitive data, failed attempts, and audit logging for security review.
The user interface for user registration and security should provide clear success, failure, and recovery messages for common scenarios.

FR-04.title: Transaction Auto-import
requirements: The platform must support transaction auto-import through stable service contracts with external systems or internal integration layers.
Transaction Auto-import should validate inbound and outbound payloads before data is accepted or shared with downstream systems.
Failures, retries, and reconciliation outcomes for transaction auto-import must be observable through logs, alerts, and operational dashboards.
Credentials, tokens, and connection settings used by transaction auto-import must be managed securely and rotated according to policy.

FR-05.title: Manual Transaction Entry
requirements: The system must support manual transaction entry as a complete business workflow within the application.
Manual Transaction Entry should validate required inputs, enforce role-based permissions, and prevent invalid state changes.
Users must be able to review the current state and recent activity associated with manual transaction entry from the relevant module.
The platform should log significant actions, failures, and user-visible events related to manual transaction entry for operational support.

FR-01.title: Plan User Registration and Security Workflow and Contracts
FR-01.description: Review the SRS requirement bullets for user registration and security and break them into executable API, UI, data, security, and reporting subtasks. Define the workflow states, user roles, field-level validations, request/response contracts, and failure cases before implementation starts. Use these requirement drivers as the baseline: Users must be able to complete user registration and security through the approved entry points provided by the platform; The system must validate submitted credentials, identity data, and required fields before allowing user registration and security to continue; User Registration and Security must enforce secure handling of sensitive data, failed attempts, and audit logging for security review.
acceptance_criteria: The end-to-end workflow for user registration and security is documented from trigger to completion
Validation rules, role restrictions, and error scenarios for user registration and security are explicitly defined
Implementation subtasks for user registration and security can be picked up independently by engineering

FR-01.title: Implement User Registration and Security Backend Services
FR-01.description: Build the backend services for user registration and security on React Native, Node.js, PostgreSQL. Create route handlers or service endpoints, model the core business operations, enforce validation and authorization rules, persist workflow state changes, and add structured error handling and audit logging. Make sure the implementation covers these behaviors: Users must be able to complete user registration and security through the approved entry points provided by the platform; The system must validate submitted credentials, identity data, and required fields before allowing user registration and security to continue; User Registration and Security must enforce secure handling of sensitive data, failed attempts, and audit logging for security review.
acceptance_criteria: Core user registration and security business rules are implemented for Personal Finance and Budgeting App
Validation, authorization, and error handling are covered for user registration and security
API/service behavior for user registration and security is testable and documented for integration

FR-01.title: Implement User Registration and Security Frontend Flow
FR-01.description: Build the frontend flow for user registration and security with the exact forms, tables, filters, wizards, status indicators, and action buttons required by the SRS. Connect the UI to the backend contract, handle optimistic updates or refresh flows where needed, and provide user-facing validation, permissions feedback, loading states, and recovery states.
acceptance_criteria: The user registration and security user flow is accessible and responsive across supported browsers
Forms, filters, tables, and feedback states for user registration and security behave correctly
The user registration and security UI is fully integrated with backend APIs and handles loading and error states

FR-01.title: Harden User Registration and Security Security and Access Rules
FR-01.description: Apply security controls to user registration and security. Implement role checks, field-level protections, data masking where appropriate, secure defaults, audit trails, and abuse-prevention rules. Verify that sensitive operations are guarded both in the API layer and in the UI visibility rules.
acceptance_criteria: Access control rules for user registration and security are enforced consistently across API and UI layers
Sensitive operations and data paths in user registration and security are audited and protected against misuse
Security validation for user registration and security passes review and automated checks

FR-01.title: Write User Registration and Security Automated and End-to-End Tests
FR-01.description: Write unit, integration, and end-to-end tests for user registration and security. Cover valid flows, invalid inputs, permission failures, state transitions, data integrity checks, and regression-prone edge cases pulled from the SRS. Add fixtures or mocks that make the feature verifiable in CI and local development.
acceptance_criteria: Happy path and failure cases for user registration and security are covered by automated tests
Permission checks and validation rules for user registration and security are verified
Regression coverage exists for the most critical user registration and security scenarios
